'''Kyrouac''' was a backwater [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Farmer/Legends|farming]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] held by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The planet's fauna included [[brush bat]]s and a [[Unidentified shelled creature|shelled creature]] residing in the [[Spiral Sea]]. The [[Kyrouaquian]] population was governed by the [[Kyrouaquian High Council]], and the planet's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Montalvia]] was the only [[City/Legends|settlement]] with a [[Montalvia spaceport|spaceport]].

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Mission to Kyrouac|sent]] a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] delegation to Kyrouac in order to convince the Kyrouaquian High Council to join the Rebellion. However, a member of the council informed the Empire about the Rebel presence on the planet, leading to a contingent of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] arriving in Montalvia aboard cargo [[Starship/Legends|starships]] used for [[Transport/Legends|transporting]] [[Grain/Legends|grain]]. The arrival of the transports was noticed by the Kyrouaquian [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] enthusiast [[CSL Danenberger]].

==Description==
Kyrouac was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Challenge" /> that was located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]] [[Kyrouac system/Legends|Kyrouac system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Vodilux sector]].<ref name="Twinkle" /> One feature of the planet's surface was the [[Spiral Sea]], which was home to a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Unidentified shelled creature|shelled creatures]]. Kyrouac's fauna also included the [[brush bat]]. Dry [[Grass/Legends|grass]] was present at the edge of a field at the [[Montalvia spaceport|spaceport]] of [[Montalvia]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]].<ref name="Challenge" />

==History==
===Imperial backwater===
Kyrouac lay at the periphery of the [[galactic community]] for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]]. The [[Republic Military/Legends|military forces]]<ref name="Challenge" /> of the [[Galactic Republic]]<ref name="Republic">{{ChallengeCite|32|Alone Against the Empire|''Alone Against the Empire''|noquotes=1}} states that, by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the adventure's events, the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] had not visited Kyrouac "even during the height of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]." The [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'' Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] later established that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was [[Declaration of a New Order|reorganized]] into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] immediately prior to the [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|end]] of the Clone Wars. This article therefore treats the aforementioned information from ''Alone Against the Empire'' as [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] applying to the Republic instead of the Empire.</ref> did not visit the world during the height of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Despite Kyrouac formally being a part of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], neither did the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] until the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], which was fought against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="Challenge" />

During the latter conflict, the Alliance, noticing Kyrouac's potential value as a source of [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] supplies, [[Mission to Kyrouac|sent]] a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] party to the [[Kyrouaquian High Council]] to discuss the possibility of the planet joining the Rebellion. When the Rebels arrived on Kyrouac, [[CSL Danenberger]], a local [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] enthusiast of the Rebellion and the history of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] tradition, unsuccessfully sought ways to approach and aid the delegation, hoping to join the Alliance that way.<ref name="Challenge" />

===Betrayal on Kyrouac===
[[File:Storm1-vd.jpg|thumb|right|195px|Imperial stormtroopers were dispatched to Kyrouac to capture Rebel diplomats.]]
As the talks between the Rebels and the Kyrouaquian High Council progressed, a member of the council contacted the Empire with information about the diplomats present on the planet. In response, an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] arrived in Kyrouac's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], and several [[Grain/Legends|grain]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]] with enough Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] aboard to take over the government of the planet's capital, Montalvia, were dispatched to the city.<ref name="Challenge" />

One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], while the talks were still ongoing, Danenberger took a walk through Montalvia, patrolling for signs of anything unusual. The youth then noticed an unusually large number of transport [[Starship/Legends|starships]]—which, unbeknownst to Danenberger, were carrying the stormtroopers—landing at the Montalvia [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]].<ref name="Challenge" />

==Inhabitants==
The [[Homeworld/Legends|natives]] of Kyrouac, known as [[Kyrouaquian]]s, [[Language/Legends|spoke]] [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] and included members of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] species. CSL Danenberger was a Human citizen of the planet whom many other Kyrouaquians considered to be crazy due to the youth's keen interest in the history of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] tradition. The population of Kyrouac was governed by the Kyrouaquian High Council and consisted mostly of [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]]. The locals [[Merchant/Legends|sold]] grain to offworlders, who then sent transport ships, including a [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]], to the Montalvia spaceport to fetch their purchased goods.<ref name="Challenge" />

The city of Montalvia was run by a local government and had its own [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|police]] as well as a service of [[Medic/Legends|medics]]. Several [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]], including a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]], were present at a local [[Montalvia cantina|cantina]] in the city during the Galactic Civil War.<ref name="Challenge" />

==Locations==
The capital city of Kyrouac was Montalvia. It was the only settlement on the planet large enough to host a spaceport, which lay off the southwestern edge of the city. During the Rebel diplomats' visit to Kyrouac, their [[Rebel lodgings|lodgings]] were located in the western section of Montalvia. The city also featured a [[Montalvia city jail|jail]], a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], and abandoned warehouses.<ref name="Challenge" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kyrouac was introduced in ''[[Alone Against the Empire]]'', a solitaire [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure written by [[Lester W. Smith]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in [[Challenge 32|''Challenge'' 32]] in [[1988]]. Since that adventure was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Challenge" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Kyrouac [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Kyrouac itself, in grid square K-14.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

===Further events on Kyrouac===
[[File:CSLDanenbergerCantina.png|thumb|right|320px|CSL Danenberger may become involved in a cantina brawl on Kyrouac in one of the ways the events of ''Alone Against the Empire'' can play out.]]
As a solitaire roleplaying adventure, ''Alone Against the Empire'' presents numerous possible scenarios for how the further events on Kyrouac involving CSL Danenberger, the character controlled by the player, unfold, depending on the player's choices. If Danenberger ignores the unusual activity at the Montalvia spaceport, the Jedi enthusiast soon visits a cantina and eventually gets involved in a brawl. The situation can result in Danenberger either getting [[Death/Legends|killed]], ending up in the local [[Prison/Legends|jail]], or succeeding in leaving the cantina. The latter two outcomes lead to the discovery that the Rebel delegation is no longer at its lodgings, and all three outcomes serve as endings for the adventure.<ref name="Challenge" />

If Danenberger investigates the landings at the spaceport, the youth can then either stealthily approach the area where the grain transports are landing or do so openly. A stealth-based approach leads to Danenberger discovering that the transports are carrying Imperial stormtroopers. At that moment, a stormtrooper may notice Danenberger, which may lead to the youth's incarceration or death. If Danenberger successfully leaves the spaceport for the Rebel lodgings, the youth then informs the delegation's [[Unidentified Rebel commander (Kyrouac)|commander]] of the developments at the spaceport, after which the Rebels, guided by Danenberger, evade Imperial capture. The Rebels and Danenberger then enter a [[Lightfight|firefight]] with stormtroopers at the spaceport in order to escape the planet aboard the delegation's starship, after which the adventure ends.<ref name="Challenge" />

Approaching the landing site of the Imperial transports openly leads to an encounter with the area's [[Security guard/Legends|security guard]], which can result in an attack on Danenberger and possible incarceration. Again, upon successfully leaving the spaceport, Danenberger heads to the Rebel lodgings, where the youth then has to convince the Rebel [[Commanding officer/Legends|commander]] of the suspicious nature of the transport landings. Success leads to both Danenberger and the Rebels witnessing the stormtroopers at the spaceport, followed by the aforementioned firefight. Failure may result in either incarceration due to the actions of a [[Unidentified Kyrouaquian official|Kyrouaquian official]] in league with the Empire or a return to the spaceport and the stealth approach to the landing site.<ref name="Challenge" />
